canon.title: FKA twigs + Yves Tumor
canon.blurb: FKA twigs brings the Body High arena tour, with Yves Tumor opening, to the 17k-cap Ziggo Dome.

canon.title: Red Light Jazz Festival
canon.blurb: Mostly-free jazz across intimate Red Light District cafés, churches and hidden rooms — 13th edition.

canon.title: Amsterdam Open Air
canon.blurb: Ten-stage open-air electronic, house and techno weekender in the park — fashion and art across the site.

canon.title: 909 Festival
canon.blurb: Outdoor techno festival in the forest with international DJs across the first weekend of June.

canon.title: Jungle by Night
canon.blurb: Amsterdam’s own brass-heavy instrumental collective plays the free open-air Vondelpark stage.

canon.title: Samora Pinderhughes
canon.blurb: Pianist-composer’s cinematic jazz and spoken-word project lands in the canalside Bimhuis.

canon.title: Humbe
canon.blurb: Mexican singer-songwriter Humbe plays his first Amsterdam show at the waterfront Noord venue.

canon.title: Jordana + Tōth
canon.blurb: Bedroom-pop songwriter Jordana makes her Amsterdam debut at Paradiso, with Tōth supporting.

canon.title: Ella Zirina Trio
canon.blurb: Latvian guitarist Ella Zirina leads her contemporary jazz trio at the Bimhuis.

canon.title: Motor City Drum Ensemble
canon.blurb: Deep-house selector MCDE headlines a DJ session at the industrial NDSM wharf.

canon.title: Arctic Futurisms with Tanya Tagaq
canon.blurb: Inuit throat-singer Tanya Tagaq headlines an evening of Arctic futurism — Friday only.

canon.title: Atomic Joy
canon.blurb: A Holland Festival theatre piece playing across the full weekend at intimate Frascati.

canon.title: Split Tooth: Saputjiji
canon.blurb: Sunday-night music-theatre adaptation of Tanya Tagaq’s novel Split Tooth.

canon.title: Ivna Esajas — Wayward Lines
canon.blurb: The ABN Amro Art Award show threads diasporic memory through installation — its very last weekend.

canon.title: Yumna Al-Arashi — Body as Resistance
canon.blurb: First solo museum show for Al-Arashi — photography, sculpture and book confront the policing of women’s bodies.

canon.title: Foam Talent 2026
canon.blurb: Fifteen emerging photographers from 107 countries — fresh on its launch weekend.

canon.title: Women in Street Art Weekend
canon.blurb: The street-art museum’s weekend spotlighting women artists, on the NDSM wharf.

canon.title: American Identities
canon.blurb: Levinthal’s staged photos and ChicanX prints pick apart the myths and power plays of American identity.

canon.title: Eye(s) Open
canon.blurb: Eleven artists confront Eye’s 2,000 colonial-era films from Indonesia and Suriname with new works.

canon.title: Danh Vo at the Stedelijk
canon.blurb: Danh Vo weaves objects, images and text into a quiet meditation on identity and human ties.

canon.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
canon.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

canon.title: De Pimpelmees
canon.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

canon.title: Sunday Market — Westergas
canon.blurb: The roaming design / food / vintage market returns to Westergas — June edition lands this Sunday.

canon.title: Noordermarkt (Saturday)
canon.blurb: Weekly Saturday antiques, vintage, books and organic-food market in the Jordaan, by the Noorderkerk.

canon.title: Amsterdam Sevens Rugby
canon.blurb: The 53rd-edition international rugby sevens — 120+ teams, fast games, a full-day atmosphere.

canon.title: Zaanse Schans windmills
canon.blurb: Eight working windmills, cheese and crafts on the Zaan — free grounds, ~17 min train from Centraal.

canon.title: Foodhallen
canon.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.

canon.title: Moeders — Dutch home cooking
canon.blurb: Walls papered with strangers’ mothers’ photos and a stamppot-and-stew menu built for a grey evening.

canon.title: Café de Klos — ribs & grill
canon.blurb: A tiny, packed grill house off the canals famous for sticky spare ribs — no frills, no reservations.

canon.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
canon.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

canon.title: Eye Filmmuseum — repertory screening
canon.blurb: Across the IJ by free ferry: a striking film museum with a daily repertory programme and a river-view café.

canon.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
canon.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.

canon.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
canon.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.

canon.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
canon.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.

canon.title: Tony’s Chocolonely Super Store
canon.blurb: The flagship of the slavery-free chocolate brand — make-your-own bars, a café, and a serious sweet-tooth detour.

canon.title: ARTIS Royal Zoo
canon.blurb: The country’s oldest zoo — big cats, an aquarium and a planetarium across a leafy 19th-century park.

canon.title: NEMO Science Museum
canon.blurb: Five floors of hands-on experiments in the big green ship — plus a free rooftop with city views.

canon.title: Micropia
canon.blurb: The world’s only microbe museum — microscopes, a kiss-o-meter and the tiny life all around us.

canon.title: Het Scheepvaartmuseum
canon.blurb: Climb aboard a full-size replica of an 18th-century VOC ship, then explore maps, globes and sea tales.

canon.title: Vondelpark playgrounds
canon.blurb: Amsterdam’s favourite park: open-air playgrounds, the Kinderkookkafé, ducks and room to run.

canon.title: Goat Farm Ridammerhoeve
canon.blurb: A working organic goat farm in the forest — bottle-feed the kids (the goat kind), then pancakes on the terrace.

canon.title: TunFun indoor playground
canon.blurb: A giant indoor adventure playground hidden in a former traffic underpass — slides, ball pits, trampolines.

canon.title: Hortus Botanicus
canon.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.

canon.title: Heritage Tram rides
canon.blurb: Ride a beautifully restored vintage tram from Haarlemmermeerstation out through the Amsterdamse Bos.

canon.title: Madurodam
canon.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

canon.title: Efteling
canon.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

canon.title: As
canon.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

canon.title: BAK
canon.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

canon.title: Brut de Mer
canon.blurb: Oysters, fish and bubbles — a tiny, lively seafood bar in De Pijp.

canon.title: Buffet van Odette
canon.blurb: A bright canal-side all-day spot for honest, seasonal cooking and very good cakes.

canon.title: Café Caron
canon.blurb: A warm neighbourhood bistro from the Caron brothers — classic French plates, done properly.

canon.title: Bar Centraal
canon.blurb: A buzzy natural-wine bar with small sharing plates on a corner in Oud-West.

canon.title: Choux
canon.blurb: Inventive, veg-forward small plates and natural wine in a raw red-brick space near the IJ.

canon.title: The Cottage
canon.blurb: Comfort food and good drinks at a snug De Pijp corner with street-side benches.

canon.title: Entrepot
canon.blurb: Seasonal sharing plates and natural wine in a light industrial hall in Amsterdam Oost.

canon.title: FC Hyena
canon.blurb: An arthouse cinema with a wine bar and a small kitchen, right on the IJ in Noord.

canon.title: Bar Fisk
canon.blurb: Sun-soaked Mediterranean seafood and sharing plates in De Pijp.

canon.title: Gebr. Hartering
canon.blurb: No menu — just whatever’s good that day. Adventurous, generous chef’s cooking by the old harbour.

canon.title: Le Hollandais
canon.blurb: Honest French-Dutch cooking — game, offal, oysters — a long-running Amsteldijk favourite.

canon.title: Kaagman & Kortekaas
canon.blurb: A tiny open-kitchen bistro doing refined, produce-led cooking in the old centre.

canon.title: Café de Klepel
canon.blurb: A candlelit wine bistro with a daily-changing French menu and a deep, characterful list.

canon.title: Café Modern
canon.blurb: A set seasonal menu in a characterful former bank across the IJ in Amsterdam Noord.

canon.title: Bar Parry
canon.blurb: A neighbourhood natural-wine bar with a short, sharp plate menu in De Pijp.

canon.title: Rijsel
canon.blurb: Lille-inspired French cooking in a converted school — that rotisserie chicken is the order.

canon.title: Scheepskameel
canon.blurb: Open-kitchen cooking and a riesling-deep list in a bright hall on Kattenburg.

canon.title: Toscanini
canon.blurb: A beloved Jordaan institution for fresh, regional Italian cooking; reserve well in advance.

canon.title: De Vrouw met de Baard
canon.blurb: An easy all-day neighbourhood spot in Oost — coffee, wine and seasonal plates.

canon.title: Wilde Zwijnen
canon.blurb: Seasonal, ingredient-driven Dutch cooking (the name means “wild boar”) in the Indische Buurt.

canon.title: Petit Gâteau
canon.blurb: Jewel-like little tarts — pistachio, lemon, chocolate — from the patisserie counter at the Foodhallen.

canon.title: The Frozen Fountain
canon.blurb: The country’s best-known design store — floors of furniture, lighting and covetable objects to browse.

canon.title: Brewery Homeland
canon.blurb: A craft brewery and beach bar on the IJ — tank-fresh beer and a waterfront sun-trap terrace.

canon.title: Libertine Petit Café
canon.blurb: A funky canal-side bar-bistro at the Noordermarkt — French classics, oysters and a stellar brunch.

canon.title: Noorderlicht
canon.blurb: A greenhouse café on the NDSM wharf — string lights, salvaged charm and a big waterside terrace.

canon.title: Café de Tuin
canon.blurb: A quintessential Jordaan brown café — newspapers, good beer and an easy neighbourhood buzz.

canon.title: The Duchess
canon.blurb: An opulent belle-époque brasserie in a former bank — marble, brass and a long oyster-and-champagne bar.

canon.title: Mata Hari
canon.blurb: A warm canal-side bar-restaurant on the Wallen — candlelit, characterful and a local antidote to the crowds.

canon.title: De Ceuvel
canon.blurb: A café on a reclaimed shipyard — a circular-economy experiment with a ramshackle, plant-filled terrace.

canon.title: Skatecafé
canon.blurb: A working skate ramp inside a former garage, with a kitchen, bar and DJs — as fun as it sounds.

canon.title: Patisserie Holtkamp
canon.blurb: A century-old patisserie behind a gorgeous Amsterdam School facade — famous for its kroketten and gateaux.

canon.title: The Movies
canon.blurb: Amsterdam’s oldest cinema — a jewel-box Art Deco screen with a candlelit dinner-and-a-film tradition.

canon.title: De Filmhallen
canon.blurb: A nine-screen arthouse cinema in a converted tram depot — with a bustling food hall right next door.

canon.title: Kriterion
canon.blurb: A legendary student-run cinema and café since 1945 — arthouse programming with a buzzy terrace.

canon.title: Sexyland World
canon.blurb: A members’ club owned by 1,000 people — a different host and a different vibe every single night.

canon.title: Garage Noord
canon.blurb: A club, kitchen and cultural space in a former garage — daytime plates, late-night dancing.

canon.title: Conservatorium Hotel
canon.blurb: The glass-roofed lounge of the city’s grandest design hotel — a stunning spot for a drink or high tea.

canon.title: Vlieger
canon.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

canon.title: Athenaeum Boekhandel
canon.blurb: The intellectual heart of the Spui — a beloved bookshop and an international newsstand packed floor-to-ceiling.

canon.title: The American Book Center
canon.blurb: A sprawling three-floor English-language bookshop — a spiral of shelves and a basement of zines and comics.

canon.title: Patta
canon.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

canon.title: Moise
canon.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

canon.title: Cowboys to Catwalk
canon.blurb: A treasure-trove of vintage designer — Margiela, Marc Jacobs and one-off finds rummaged from decades of fashion.

canon.title: Mobilia
canon.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

canon.title: X Bank
canon.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.

canon.title: Friday Next
canon.blurb: A concept store and café in one — young Dutch design to browse, with brunch and coffee while you do it.

canon.title: WonderWood
canon.blurb: A gallery-shop devoted to plywood and mid-century modern — bentwood chairs and design classics since 1999.

canon.title: RIJKS®
canon.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk’s Michelin-starred dining room beside the Rijksmuseum — the “cuisine of the Low Countries”, done with flair.

canon.title: Fromagerie Abraham Kef
canon.blurb: A legendary French cheese shop — sit at the back for a board of raw-milk cheeses and a glass of wine.

canon.title: Café Jakarta
canon.blurb: Indonesian rijsttafel under a soaring, palm-filled atrium at Hotel Jakarta — a jungle dining room out on the IJ.

canon.title: Rijksmuseum
canon.blurb: The Netherlands’ treasure house — Rembrandt’s Night Watch, Vermeer and the Golden Age masters in a cathedral of a building.

canon.title: Stedelijk Museum
canon.blurb: Modern and contemporary art behind the “bathtub” facade — Malevich, De Kooning, Appel and bold rotating shows.

canon.title: Foam
canon.blurb: A nimble photography museum in a canal house — big-name retrospectives and emerging talent, always worth the drop-in.

canon.title: Huis Marseille
canon.blurb: The city’s first photography museum, across two canal houses with a hidden garden — beautifully curated, never crowded.

canon.title: Upstream Gallery
canon.blurb: A contemporary gallery with a digital streak — internet-age artists like Rafaël Rozendaal and Jonas Lund.

canon.title: TonTon Club
canon.blurb: An arcade bar where you pay to play — pinball, classic cabinets and Japanese games, with beer and gyoza on tap.

canon.title: Albert Cuypmarkt
canon.blurb: The Netherlands’ biggest street market — 250-odd stalls of stroopwafels, cheese, fabric and flowers the length of De Pijp.

canon.title: Waterlooplein Flea Market
canon.blurb: The city’s oldest flea market — secondhand everything, vintage denim, records and bric-a-brac spread out beside the Stopera.

canon.title: IJ-Hallen
canon.blurb: Europe’s biggest flea market — 750 stalls across two NDSM shipyard warehouses, a proper treasure-hunt of vintage and junk.

canon.title: Dappermarkt
canon.blurb: A buzzing, multicultural Oost street market — spices, textiles and world food at honest neighbourhood prices.

canon.title: Pure Markt
canon.blurb: An artisan food-and-craft market in a green park — small makers, slow food and a relaxed Sunday-stroll crowd in Frankendael.

canon.title: Bloemenmarkt
canon.blurb: The world’s only floating flower market — barges of tulips, bulbs and seeds moored along the Singel since 1862.

canon.title: Utrecht
canon.blurb: Amsterdam’s quieter little sister — wharf-side canals, the soaring Dom Tower and a real café culture, minus the crowds.

canon.title: Marken & Volendam
canon.blurb: Two former Zuiderzee fishing villages — wooden houses, working harbours and smoked eel, an easy half-day from Centraal.

canon.title: Zandvoort Beach
canon.blurb: The nearest North Sea beach — wide sands, beach clubs and a direct train from Centraal; in summer half the city decamps here.

canon.title: Muiderslot
canon.blurb: A moated medieval castle on the IJmeer — turrets, falconry and a walled garden, the best-preserved fortress in the country.

canon.title: Kinderdijk Windmills
canon.blurb: Nineteen 18th-century windmills lined along the polders — the postcard Dutch landscape, a UNESCO site you can walk or cycle.

canon.title: Paradiso
canon.blurb: Amsterdam’s legendary music hall in a converted church — stained glass overhead, a heaving floor below, indie to techno.

canon.title: Melkweg
canon.blurb: A former dairy turned multi-room culture machine — live music, club nights, film and a gallery, all under one roof.

canon.title: Royal Concertgebouw
canon.blurb: One of the world’s great concert halls — peerless acoustics, a resident world-class orchestra, and free Wednesday lunch concerts.

canon.title: Strand van Oost
canon.blurb: A beach on a roof in Oost: sand-and-sunset bar on top of Orchid House that makes the city briefly disappear.

canon.title: Wils at the Farm
canon.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk and Thomas Val cook open-fire dinners at the farm that grows their ingredients — long tables, meadow views.

canon.title: Mr. Watson
canon.blurb: No meat, no fish, but plenty of fire: mushrooms roasted over open flame plus cocktails in a 'botanical roast room'.

canon.title: Kama Handroll Bar
canon.blurb: The team behind two-star 212 goes casual: a tight, fast temaki counter for precision handrolls on the Utrechtsestraat.

canon.title: Bouillon d'Amsterdam
canon.blurb: The Paris-style bouillon formula — fast, French, absurdly affordable — doubles down with a second canteen in Zuid.

canon.title: Bar Staal
canon.blurb: All-day Mediterranean-Asian fusion bar in the fast-changing Schinkel quarter — the neighborhood's newcomer worth crossing town for.

canon.title: Rotate
canon.blurb: From the Jondani crew: French-Asian shared dining with a serious wine focus, built for evenings you don't want to end.

canon.title: De Baanderij
canon.blurb: The old IJ-Kantine reborn: French-Mediterranean all-day kitchen with a huge waterside terrace facing the ferry — melon gazpacho to late dinners.

canon.title: REM Eiland
canon.blurb: The offshore broadcast platform relaunches as restaurant, rooftop and social bar in one — Scandinavian-leaning menu, golden-hour views over the water.

canon.title: Tacite
canon.blurb: Young chefs with Michelin-kitchen CVs making fine dining feel exciting again — ambitious plates without the starch or the stuffiness.

canon.title: TÊTE
canon.blurb: Affordable all-day bistro on the water in Oost — carafes of wine, easy plates, and dinners that quietly stretch to three hours.

canon.title: Warung Spang Makandra
canon.blurb: Surinamese-Javanese counter where the roti and bami outclass restaurants triple the price. A De Pijp constant since 1978.

canon.title: Bakers & Roasters
canon.blurb: Kiwi-Brazilian brunch institution: banana bread, proper flat whites and benedicts that still set the city's weekend standard.

canon.title: De Kas
canon.blurb: Chefs cook what the on-site nursery grew that morning, served inside a 1926 municipal greenhouse. Amsterdam's greenest dinner ritual.

canon.title: Brouwerij 't IJ
canon.blurb: The brewery under the De Gooyer windmill: Zatte and ossenworst on a terrace that invented the Amsterdam craft-beer afternoon.

canon.title: Glou Glou
canon.blurb: The bar that taught De Pijp to drink natural: a chalkboard of funky glasses, cheese plates, and standing-room spill onto the square.

canon.title: Droog
canon.blurb: Where Dutch conceptual design was born: gallery, shop, courtyard garden and a single hotel room in a Staalstraat warehouse.

canon.title: Rush Hour Records
canon.blurb: Globally revered house and techno shop and label — the racks where Amsterdam's DJ lineage actually gets built.

canon.title: MENDO
canon.blurb: A self-described candy store for book aficionados: black-walled cabinet of photography, design and fashion monographs.

canon.title: Red Light Records
canon.blurb: Crate den in a former red-light window: staff-picked obscurities, listening decks, zero algorithm. Ring the bell.

canon.title: Museum Van Loon
canon.blurb: A patrician canal house still furnished as lived: portraits, coach house, and the quietest formal garden inside the ring.

canon.title: Amsterdamse Bos
canon.blurb: A hand-planted 1930s forest three times Central Park's size: canoes, an open-air theatre and hidden meadows, twenty minutes from De Pijp.

canon.title: Nxt Museum
canon.blurb: Warehouse-scale new-media museum in Noord: room-sized audiovisual installations rewired each season by artists and coders.
